content: read reviews/688.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/688.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/688.md.

    19→- [x] Optional: includes cross-references to related problems #687 and #689.
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos688 ... end Erdos688`.
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos688`.
    25→- [x] No `open`/`open scoped` statements needed; none present. N/A.
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] Helper `def PrimeCoveringInRange` has a LaTeX docstring (lines 38–40).
    30→- [x] No classical choice or nonconstructive operations — definition returns `Prop` via decidable arithmetic; no `noncomputable` needed.
    31→- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
    32→- [x] Type/Prop uses `UpperCamelCase`: `PrimeCoveringInRange`.
    33→- [x] `local notation` not used — not needed here. N/A.
    34→- [x] No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks — optional. N/A.
    35→
    36→## 6. Theorem Statements
    37→
    38→- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
    39→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_688` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    40→- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
    41→- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line:
    42→  - `erdos_688`: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`
    43→  - `erdos_688.variants.lower_bound`: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`
    44→- [x] Main theorem and variant both have docstrings.
    45→- [x] Docstrings describe the mathematical content. No specific paper citations (e.g., `[Er95]`), but the module docstring attributes the lower bound to Erdős directly — acceptable.
    46→
    47→### `answer()` usage
    48→
    49→The problem asks "is it true that εₙ = o(1)?" — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).
    50→
    51→- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
    52→- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: `answer(True)` would mean "yes, εₙ = o(1)", i.e., for any δ > 0 and sufficiently large n, no covering with primes in (n^δ, n] exists (meaning εₙ < δ eventually).
    53→- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ δ`, `∃ N₀`, `∀ n`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
    54→
    55→## 7. Category Attribute
    56→
    57→- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
    58→- [x] Values are valid: `research open` (main theorem) and `research solved` (variant).
    59→- [x] The `research open` theorem (`erdos_688`) contains `sorry` in its proof body.
    60→
    61→## 8. AMS Attribute
    62→
    63→- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
    64→- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `11`.
    65→- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry — trivially satisfied).
    66→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    67→- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Number theory).
    68→
    69→## 9. Proof Bodies
    70→
    71→- [x] Open conjecture (`erdos_688`) uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` — acceptable per checklist.
    72→- [x] Solved variant also uses `by\n  sorry` (proof not yet formalized) — acceptable.
    73→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    74→- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems.
    75→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    76→
    77→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    78→
    79→- [x] Variant named `erdos_688.variants.lower_bound` — follows `erdos_N.variants.<descriptor>` convention.
    80→- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
    81→- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 11]` attributes.
    82→- [x] Variant has a docstring describing the known lower bound εₙ ≫ (log log log n)/(log log n).
    83→
    84→## 11. Code Quality
    85→
    86→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    87→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `≤`, `≥`, `¬`, `↔`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`.
    88→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    89→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    90→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    91→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    92→
    93→## 12. Build Verification
    94→
    95→- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
    96→- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.
    97→
    98→---
    99→
   100→**Overall:** The file passes all style and content checks. The formalization cleanly captures both the main conjecture (εₙ = o(1)?) and the known lower bound as a variant. The `answer()` usage correctly identifies the yes/no nature of the problem. No issues found.

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/688.lean` — Erdős Problem 688

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–34).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 688`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/688](https://www.erdosproblems.com/688)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines εₙ, states the estimation question, mentions Erdős's known lower bound).
- [x] Optional: includes cross-references to related problems #687 and #689.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos688 ... end Erdos688`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos688`.
- [x] No `open`/`open scoped` statements needed; none present. N/A.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper `def PrimeCoveringInRange` has a LaTeX docstring (lines 38–40).
- [x] No classical choice or nonconstructive operations — definition returns `Prop` via decidable arithmetic; no `noncomputable` needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [x] Type/Prop uses `UpperCamelCase`: `PrimeCoveringInRange`.
- [x] `local notation` not used — not needed here. N/A.
- [x] No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks — optional. N/A.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_688` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line:
  - `erdos_688`: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`
  - `erdos_688.variants.lower_bound`: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`
- [x] Main theorem and variant both have docstrings.
- [x] Docstrings describe the mathematical content. No specific paper citations (e.g., `[Er95]`), but the module docstring attributes the lower bound to Erdős directly — acceptable.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks "is it true that εₙ = o(1)?" — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: `answer(True)` would mean "yes, εₙ = o(1)", i.e., for any δ > 0 and sufficiently large n, no covering with primes in (n^δ, n] exists (meaning εₙ < δ eventually).
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ δ`, `∃ N₀`, `∀ n`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Values are valid: `research open` (main theorem) and `research solved` (variant).
- [x] The `research open` theorem (`erdos_688`) contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `11`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry — trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Number theory).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture (`erdos_688`) uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` — acceptable per checklist.
- [x] Solved variant also uses `by\n  sorry` (proof not yet formalized) — acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant named `erdos_688.variants.lower_bound` — follows `erdos_N.variants.<descriptor>` convention.
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 11]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring describing the known lower bound εₙ ≫ (log log log n)/(log log n).

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `≤`, `≥`, `¬`, `↔`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.

---

**Overall:** The file passes all style and content checks. The formalization cleanly captures both the main conjecture (εₙ = o(1)?) and the known lower bound as a variant. The `answer()` usage correctly identifies the yes/no nature of the problem. No issues found.

content.text: The review passed. The summary says "The file passes all style and content checks" with "No issues found." No changes needed.
